Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -172,7 +172,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1i67.1","title":"Timeline Data Structure","description":"Sequence of animation events with timing.

## Requirements
- Timeline with keyed events
- Absolute and relative timing
- Loop and repeat support
- Pause/resume/seek control

## Proposed API

```rust
pub struct Timeline<M> {
    events: Vec<TimelineEvent<M>>,
    duration: Duration,
    loop_count: LoopCount,
    state: PlaybackState,
    current_time: Duration,
}

pub struct TimelineEvent<M> {
    pub offset: Duration,       // When event starts
    pub duration: Duration,     // How long it runs
    pub animation: Box<dyn Animation<M>>,
    pub label: Option<String>,  // For seeking by name
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
pub enum LoopCount {
    Once,
    Times(u32),
    Infinite,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
pub enum PlaybackState {
    Idle,
    Playing,
    Paused,
    Finished,
}

impl<M> Timeline<M> {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    
    /// Add event at absolute offset
    pub fn add(self, offset: Duration, anim: impl Animation<M>) -> Self;
    
    /// Add event relative to last event's end
    pub fn then(self, anim: impl Animation<M>) -> Self;
    
    /// Add event with label
    pub fn add_labeled(self, label: &str, offset: Duration, anim: impl Animation<M>) -> Self;
    
    /// Set loop behavior
    pub fn loop_count(self, count: LoopCount) -> Self;
    
    /// Playback control
    pub fn play(&mut self);
    pub fn pause(&mut self);
    pub fn resume(&mut self);
    pub fn stop(&mut self);
    pub fn seek(&mut self, time: Duration);
    pub fn seek_label(&mut self, label: &str);
    
    /// Process tick, return messages
    pub fn tick(&mut self, delta: Duration) -> Vec<M>;
    
    /// Current progress 0.0 - 1.0
    pub fn progress(&self) -> f32;
    
    /// Check if complete
    pub fn is_finished(&self) -> bool;
}
```

## Implementation Details

### Event Scheduling
Store events sorted by offset. On each tick:
1. Advance current_time by delta
2. Find events that should be active (offset <= current_time < offset + duration)
3. Call tick() on each active animation
4. Collect messages

### Looping
When current_time >= duration and loop_count allows:
- Reset current_time to 0
- Decrement loop counter (if finite)
- Reset all animations

## File: crates/ftui-extras/src/animation/timeline.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_sequential_events: A then B then C
- test_overlapping_events: Parallel animations
- test_loop_finite: Loops exact count
- test_loop_infinite: Never finishes
- test_seek_time: Jump to specific time
- test_seek_label: Jump to labeled event
- test_pause_resume: State preservation

## Snapshot Tests
- timeline_progress_0_25_50_75_100: Key frames

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Timeline Data Structure
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile; include isomorphism proof for perf changes
- Unit tests: edge cases + regression fixtures; property tests for invariants where applicable
- E2E: deterministic mode + verbose JSONL logs (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums)

 {"id":"bd-1iqn","title":"Render Budget Enforcement with Graceful Degradation","description":"# Render Budget Enforcement with Graceful Degradation

## Background & Motivation

The plan defines performance budgets (1ms p50 for Present 80x24), but these are **observational** - we measure and regress-test. This bead adds **enforcement** - the system actively prevents budget overruns by degrading gracefully.

**Why this matters for agent harnesses:**
- Agent UIs receive unpredictable content (burst log output, large tool responses)
- A frozen UI during burst input makes the agent feel broken
- Users tolerate reduced visual fidelity; they do NOT tolerate hangs
- Self-regulating performance eliminates the need for manual tuning

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Complements bd-3aqs (output bytes tracking) - that measures, this controls
- Complements bd-19x (benchmarks) - benchmarks detect regressions, this prevents runtime issues
- Extends bd-10i.4.3 (Presenter) - Presenter gains budget awareness

## Technical Design

### Core Types

```rust
/// Render time budget with graceful degradation
pub struct RenderBudget {
    /// Total time budget for this frame
    total: Duration,
    /// When this frame started
    start: Instant,
    /// Current degradation level
    degradation: DegradationLevel,
    /// Per-phase budgets (diff, present, widget-render)
    phase_budgets: PhaseBudgets,
}

/// Progressive degradation levels
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
pub enum DegradationLevel {
    Full = 0,           // All visual features enabled
    SimpleBorders = 1,  // Unicode box-drawing to ASCII
    NoStyling = 2,      // Colors to monochrome
    EssentialOnly = 3,  // Skip decorative widgets
    Skeleton = 4,       // Just layout boxes
    SkipFrame = 5,      // Emergency: skip frame entirely
}

impl DegradationLevel {
    pub fn next(&self) -> Self {
        match self {
            Self::Full => Self::SimpleBorders,
            Self::SimpleBorders => Self::NoStyling,
            Self::NoStyling => Self::EssentialOnly,
            Self::EssentialOnly => Self::Skeleton,
            Self::Skeleton | Self::SkipFrame => Self::SkipFrame,
        }
    }
}
```

### Budget API

```rust
impl RenderBudget {
    pub fn new(total: Duration) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn remaining(&self) -> Duration {
        self.total.saturating_sub(self.start.elapsed())
    }
    pub fn should_degrade(&self, estimated_cost: Duration) -> bool {
        self.remaining() < estimated_cost
    }
    pub fn degrade(&mut self) { self.degradation = self.degradation.next(); }
    pub fn degradation(&self) -> DegradationLevel { self.degradation }
    pub fn exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        self.remaining().is_zero() || self.degradation == DegradationLevel::SkipFrame
    }
}
```

### Widget Integration

Widgets check degradation level and adjust rendering:

```rust
impl Widget for Panel {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, budget: &RenderBudget) {
        match budget.degradation() {
            DegradationLevel::Full => {
                self.render_unicode_border(area, buf);
                self.render_title_styled(area, buf);
            }
            DegradationLevel::SimpleBorders => {
                self.render_ascii_border(area, buf);  // +- instead of box chars
                self.render_title_styled(area, buf);
            }
            DegradationLevel::NoStyling => {
                self.render_ascii_border(area, buf);
                self.render_title_plain(area, buf);   // No colors
            }
            DegradationLevel::EssentialOnly => {
                self.render_content_only(area, buf);
            }
            DegradationLevel::Skeleton => {
                buf.fill_rect(area, Cell::new(' '));
            }
            DegradationLevel::SkipFrame => {}
        }
    }
}
```

### Recovery Mechanism

```rust
impl RenderBudget {
    pub fn should_upgrade(&self) -> bool {
        // If >50% budget remaining, try upgrading next frame
        self.remaining() > self.total / 2 && self.degradation != DegradationLevel::Full
    }
    pub fn upgrade(&mut self) {
        self.degradation = match self.degradation {
            DegradationLevel::SkipFrame => DegradationLevel::Skeleton,
            DegradationLevel::Skeleton => DegradationLevel::EssentialOnly,
            DegradationLevel::EssentialOnly => DegradationLevel::NoStyling,
            DegradationLevel::NoStyling => DegradationLevel::SimpleBorders,
            _ => DegradationLevel::Full,
        };
    }
}
```

### Configuration

```rust
pub struct FrameBudgetConfig {
    pub total: Duration,           // default: 16ms (~60fps feel)
    pub diff_budget: Duration,     // default: 2ms
    pub present_budget: Duration,  // default: 4ms
    pub allow_frame_skip: bool,    // default: true
    pub degradation_cooldown: u32, // frames between level changes
}
```

## Test Plan

### Unit Tests
- [ ] RenderBudget::remaining() correctly computes time left
- [ ] DegradationLevel ordering is correct (Full < SkipFrame)
- [ ] should_degrade() returns true when cost > remaining
- [ ] Upgrade/downgrade cycle is symmetric and bounded
- [ ] Default config values are sensible

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Widget renders at all degradation levels without panic
- [ ] Frame loop respects budget.exhausted()
- [ ] Recovery mechanism restores full fidelity after burst

### Property Tests
- [ ] Degradation level never exceeds SkipFrame
- [ ] Budget remaining is always >= 0
- [ ] With infinite budget, degradation stays at Full

### E2E Tests
```bash
./ftui-harness --test-mode burst-logs --log-rate 10000/s --duration 5s
# Assert: no frames take > 2x budget
# Assert: UI remains responsive (input latency < 100ms)
```

## Logging Requirements

All degradation events logged at WARN level with frame number, from/to levels, and trigger phase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RenderBudget type implemented in ftui-render
- [ ] DegradationLevel enum with 6 levels
- [ ] All harness-essential widgets respect degradation
- [ ] Frame loop integrates budget checking
- [ ] Recovery mechanism prevents permanent degradation
- [ ] Unit tests pass with 100% coverage of public API
- [ ] E2E burst test shows no >2x budget overruns","notes":"## Missing API: Frame Reset

The RenderBudget needs a method to reset for new frames:

```rust
impl RenderBudget {
    /// Reset budget for a new frame
    /// Called at the start of each render cycle
    pub fn new_frame(&mut self) {
        self.start = Instant::now();
        // Don't reset degradation - let it recover naturally via should_upgrade()
    }
    
    /// Force reset to full quality (for testing or user override)
    pub fn reset_to_full(&mut self) {
        self.degradation = DegradationLevel::Full;
        self.start = Instant::now();
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-4kq0.5.3","title":"Knuth-Plass Tests + Perf Gates","description":"## Goal
Validate correctness and ensure performance stays within budget.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- property_dp_vs_greedy: DP never worse in total badness.
- snapshot_wrap_quality: known paragraphs at multiple widths.
- perf_wrap_bench: p50/p95 logged with thresholds.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- PTY replay of wrapped paragraphs across widths; log JSONL with seed, badness, line breaks.
- Deterministic mode for consistent logs and snapshots.

## JSONL Schema (E2E)
- ts: RFC3339 timestamp.
- seed: u64.
- width: u16.
- lines: u32.
- badness_total: f64.
- algorithm: "greedy" | "dp".
- wrap_time_us: u64.
- breaks_checksum: hex string.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E2E logs include width, breakpoints, badness, and timing metrics.
- [ ] Snapshot diffs are reviewable and deterministic.
- [ ] Perf budgets enforced via JSONL logs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:20:35.333949721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:20:54.184437142Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:20:54.184417856Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:20:35.333949721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:22:42.988892778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-4kq0.6","title":"WidthCache: W-TinyLFU + Collision Guard","description":"## Goal
Improve width cache hit rate and correctness by using a modern admission policy (W‑TinyLFU) and collision guards.

## Core Idea
- Use a Count‑Min Sketch + Doorkeeper to estimate frequency (TinyLFU).\n- Admit new entries only if they exceed frequency of victims.\n- Add collision guard: store (hash, len, short fingerprint) to avoid false hits.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hit rate improves on realistic workloads.\n- Collision probability effectively zero in practice.\n- No hot‑path allocations.

## Subtasks
- TinyLFU math spec + parameters\n- Implementation (count‑min + LRU window)\n- Tests + perf regression","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:20:42.495235989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:20:42.495235989Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.6","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:20:42.495235989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-4kq0.6.1","title":"TinyLFU Spec (Count-Min + Doorkeeper)","description":"## Goal
Define parameters for W‑TinyLFU and collision guard strategy.

## Math Spec
- Count‑Min Sketch with width w, depth d; error <= epsilon*N with prob 1−delta.
- Doorkeeper: 1‑bit Bloom to filter one‑off items.
- Admission rule: admit if freq(candidate) > freq(victim).

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_cms_bounds: observed error within epsilon.
- unit_admission_rule: higher‑freq wins.
- unit_fingerprint_guard: collisions detected and rejected.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parameters (w, d, epsilon, delta) are chosen with explicit bounds and justified defaults.
- [ ] Admission/eviction rules are fully specified and implementable without ambiguity.
- [ ] Collision guard behavior is specified and covered by unit tests.

-{"id":"bd-4kq0.6.2","title":"TinyLFU Implementation (WidthCache)","description":"## Goal
Implement W‑TinyLFU inside `WidthCache` with a small window LRU and main cache.

## Implementation Notes
- Window cache (LRU) for recent items.
- Main cache (SLRU) for frequent items.
- Count‑Min sketch updated on accesses.
- Fingerprint guard stored with hash.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_window_promotion: frequent items move to main cache.
- perf_cache_hit_rate: compare vs baseline on replay workload.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implementation matches the spec (window + main cache + admission policy) and is deterministic.
- [ ] Collision guard prevents wrong-width returns under hash collision scenarios.
- [ ] Perf shows improvement on representative workloads and is logged as JSONL.

 {"id":"bd-4kq0.6.3","title":"WidthCache Tests + Perf Gates","description":"## Goal
Ensure correctness and performance improvements with regression gates.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- property_cache_equivalence: cached width equals computed width.
- e2e_cache_replay: deterministic workload logs hit rate + latency.
- perf_cache_overhead: cache operations < 1us p95.

## JSONL Schema (E2E)
- ts: RFC3339 timestamp.
- seed: u64.
- workload_id: string.
- hits: u64.
- misses: u64.
- hit_rate: f64.
- size: u32.
- op_time_us_p50: u64.
- op_time_us_p95: u64.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cached width is always equal to computed width (property tests, deterministic seeds).
- [ ] E2E replay logs hit rate and latency (JSONL) and supports deterministic reproduction.
- [ ] Perf overhead meets budget (< 1us p95) on representative environments.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:00.449753265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:40:59.139793685Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:00.449753265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:22:49.993560614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-4kq0.7","title":"Terminal Capabilities: Runtime Probing + Bayesian Evidence","description":"## Goal
Upgrade capability detection from static env inference to runtime probing with formal confidence and timeouts.

## Core Idea
- Probe capabilities (sync output, OSC8, clipboard) via bounded queries.\n- Use Bayesian evidence to decide enable/disable and record confidence.\n- Fail‑open: if probe times out, keep conservative defaults.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Probing never blocks more than configured timeout.\n- Evidence ledger exposes decision and confidence.\n- Deterministic behavior under fixed env + mock responses.

## Subtasks
- Probe protocol + timeout design\n- Bayesian decision rule + confidence ledger\n- Tests + E2E harness with mocked terminals","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:07.406215212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:07.406215212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.7","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:07.406215212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-4kq0.7.1","title":"Capability Probe Spec + Timeouts","description":"## Goal
Define probe requests, response parsing, and strict timeout policy.

## Spec
- For each capability, define probe message and expected response.
- Timeout budget per probe (e.g., 20ms) and global cap.
- Fail‑open with conservative defaults.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_timeout_enforced: probe aborts within budget.
- unit_parse_response: valid/invalid responses handled.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Probe protocol is specified per capability (request, expected response, parse rules).
- [ ] Timeout budgets are enforced and tested; failure modes are explicit (fail-open behavior).
- [ ] Parsing is robust (invalid/malformed responses handled deterministically).

## E2E Coverage
- Covered by `bd-4kq0.7.3` (mock terminal harness + JSONL logs).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:13.521989133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:25:55.976306167Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:13.521989133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
